frameworks.name: domain-specificity of metacognition
frameworks.one_line: tests whether memory and perceptual metacognition rely on dissociable cortical substrates

frameworks.name: metacognitive efficiency
frameworks.one_line: behavioural index of introspective monitoring quantified across decision domains

frameworks.name: repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation perturbation
frameworks.one_line: causal disruption of precuneus to test functional necessity for mnemonic metacognition

frameworks.name: anterior prefrontal lesion evidence
frameworks.one_line: prior lesion work implicating aPFC in perceptual but not mnemonic metacognition

core_question: Is the precuneus causally necessary for mnemonic but not perceptual metacognition?
key_assumptions: metacognitive efficiency can be measured separately for memory and perceptual decisions
focal rTMS over precuneus selectively perturbs that region's processing
domain-general vs domain-specific metacognition is dissociable across cortical sites
manipulations: task domain: temporal-order memory judgment vs perceptual discrimination
key_findings: precuneal perturbation impaired metacognitive efficiency for memory but not perception
cross-domain correlation in individuals' metacognitive efficiency disappeared after precuneus rTMS
interpretations: precuneus is functionally necessary for mnemonic metacognition specifically
memory and perceptual metacognition are double-dissociated across precuneus and anterior prefrontal cortex
